questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Consider a parabola $y^2=\alpha x$ and a point $\left(-\frac{\alpha}{4}, 0\right)$ then midpoint of centres of the circles touching the tangents from given point and its chord of contact is
questions.solution:
$\left(-\frac{\alpha}{4}, 0\right)$ lies on directrix and centre be $\left(x_1, 0\right)$. Equation of one of the tangent is $x+y+\frac{\alpha}{4}=0$
and chord is $x-\frac{\alpha}{4}=0$, centre is $\left(x_1, 0\right)$
$\frac{\left|x_1+\frac{\alpha}{4}\right|}{\sqrt{2}}=\frac{\left|x_1-\frac{\alpha}{4}\right|}{1} \Rightarrow x_1^2+\frac{\alpha^2}{16}+\frac{\alpha x}{2}=2 x_1^2+\frac{\alpha^2}{8}-\alpha x$
$x_1^2-\frac{3 \alpha x}{2}+\frac{\alpha^2}{16}=0 \Rightarrow x_1+x_2=\frac{3 \alpha}{2} \Rightarrow$ Midpoint $\equiv\left(\frac{3 \alpha}{4}, 0\right)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.q: Consider a parabola $x^2=4 y$ and a hyperbola $x y=1 . A$ tangent is drawn to parabola meets the hyperbola in $A$ and $B$ then locus of midpoint of $A B$ is
questions.solution:
Let midpoint is $(h, k)$ then chord is $y=-\frac{h}{k} x+2 h$ and if it is a tangent then $x^2=4\left(-\frac{h}{k} x+2 h\right)$
$D=0$
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{k}^2=-\frac{\mathrm{h}}{2}$ (Parabola)
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
